summary: Removed two redundant self-typedefs of Vec3 from a shared header and a stage-related C file because the typedef did nothing and caused issues for m2c.
what_changed: Deleted `typedef Vec3 Vec3;` from `include/common_structs.h` near `struct DemoMotionFiles` and `union _UnkFlagStruct` definitions.
Deleted `typedef Vec3 Vec3;` from `src/melee/gr/grdynamicattr.c` near the file includes and before `struct _UnkStruct`.
No functional code was added; the PR only removed four lines across two files.
smart_moves: Removed a no-op typedef instead of preserving redundant compatibility clutter.
Addressed a decompilation tooling issue directly: the PR body states that the self-typedef caused issues for m2c.
Kept the patch minimal, reducing risk in common headers and stage code.
decomp_lessons: Avoid self-referential typedef aliases such as `typedef Vec3 Vec3;`; they provide no type information and may confuse decompilation tooling.
When m2c or related tooling has trouble with a declaration, prefer deleting redundant declarations rather than adding workaround aliases unless evidence shows the alias is required.
Shared headers like `include/common_structs.h` should stay clean because redundant declarations can affect many translation units.
Small type/header cleanups can be valuable when they improve tool compatibility even without changing matching code.
naming_conventions: `Vec3` was already an available type name and did not need to be re-typedefed to itself.
The removed alias confirms that redundant same-name typedefs are not a desired convention in this codebase.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic was shown in the diff.
The relevant decomp tactic was tooling hygiene: remove a declaration that m2c mishandles instead of relying on a redundant typedef.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR was reviewed and merged quickly, but the excerpt does not include substantive reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find other occurrences of `typedef X X;` that may be redundant or problematic for m2c.
Search for m2c-related cleanup PRs that removed or simplified typedefs.
Check whether `Vec3` is defined consistently in shared headers and included where needed without local self-typedefs.
